title: SHA-256 state struct not zeroed after use in generate_eui64
description:
In lora_l2.c generate_eui64(), the hash output buffer is zeroed (line 119), but the tc_sha256_state_struct containing intermediate hash state is not zeroed.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:98, 119

Code:
- Line 98: struct tc_sha256_state_struct sha_state; (on stack)
- Line 119: memset(hash, 0, sizeof(hash)); (zeroes output, not state)

What goes wrong: The sha_state structure contains intermediate hash values that could theoretically be used in a side-channel attack to recover information about the hardware ID. On embedded systems with limited memory protection, stack contents may persist and be observable.

This is defense-in-depth for cryptographic hygiene. The risk is low since hwid is not high-entropy secret material, but the pattern differs from ipv6_addr.c:lichen_pubkey_to_iid() which does zero its hash buffer.

Fix: Add memset(&sha_state, 0, sizeof(sha_state)) after tc_sha256_final().
